raw_consumer_statement: On the evening of XXXX XXXX, I started getting very bizarre and frequent ( Every XXXX seconds ) emails. I was at a holiday party in XXXXXXXX XXXXXXXX A friend at the party suspected that I was getting " email bombed '' I then went home and checked my bank and all looked good. The next morning, I received a text from my XXXX XXXX Barclays Credit Card questioning a charge from XXXX for {$460.00}. Apparently an email from XXXX had come through in the midst of the XXXX or so emails I got that evening ( I found this out later ). I immediately called the credit card company and notified them that the charge, along with another from XXXX for {$820.00} were both fraudulent. The charges were flagged, my card was closed, and I was issued a new card. a few days later, I got am email from XXXX asking how my check-in had gone. I realized that someone had actually checked into a hotel under the booking that was made on my credit card! I immediately contacted the credit card company as I assumed the charges had been denied as I had notified them they were not charges I made. The person I spoke to did not seem interested in helping and very casually told me I had to contact the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX She connected me with them. I spent XXXX min on hold before hanging up. I found out later they were closed for the day. Approximately XXXX month or so later, I noticed the XXXX charges had re-appeared on my credit card statement. I then called the credit card company and they stated that because my email had been associated with the charges, they reassigned the charges to me. I vehemently disputed the charges and asked what I could do to prove that I did not make those charges. I have since called the fraud investigation team twice and spoken to a supervisor. They created a case file for me and said it would take XXXX billing cycles to resolve. I have heard nothing from them and it has been almost XXXX days. They have provided me with no means of proving that I was not in the area when the charge was made, nor did I stay in the hotel or check into it. I did call booking.com and they agreed that the name on the hotel booking was not me. I have done everything I can to resolve this but seem to be hitting a wall with the credit card company. They will not listen to my story and also will not provide me any guidance as to how to prove I did not make these charges. The supervisor I spoke to was XXXX on XX/XX/year>. She gave me XXXX claim numbers, XXXX for each charge. The first one is XXXX. The second is XXXX. I have called the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX at XXXX. They have given me a second number as well XXXX. I hope you can help me. Thank you!
